title: Misleading free trial and unauthorized charges
description: Multiple users report being charged immediately or immediately after a short period despite the app promising a free trial. Some users were charged after cancelling their subscription, and customer support is often unresponsive or marks inquiries as spam. This leads to financial loss and loss of trust.
supporting_excerpts: I was told in app that there would be no charge until the end of the free trial and you guys immediately charged me!
it said it was giving me a free subscription ... it's charged me 30 dollars
once I used the Apple Pay feature to subscribe to the free trial I was charged $29.99!?
You told me a free trial for a week and the charged me $19.99 right away.
They offer a free week then when you accept they charge you.
I was charged immediately despite free trial claim and cannot get a refund.
Cancelled my subscription and then received not one but two charges in the same day totaling almost $100!!!
Cancelled subscription and still got charged for it. No spot in app to manage no place on website either.
assumptions: Users expect a genuine free trial period without immediate charges.
Some users may have misread terms, but the volume of reports suggests a systemic issue.
Billing after cancellation indicates a possible bug or deceptive practice.
uncertainty_notes: Some users may have inadvertently agreed to immediate charges, but the pattern across multiple reviews strongly indicates misleading trial design.

title: Aggressive paywall and loss of previously free features
description: Long-time users report that the app has transitioned from a free or mostly free model to requiring a subscription for almost all features. Workouts previously accessible are now locked behind a paywall, leading to frustration and a sense of bait-and-switch.
supporting_excerpts: EVERYTHING requires money , everything is behind a paywall..
All of a sudden I couldn’t access anything without paying an outrageous price.
hate how they kept going from completely free to charging for everything now.
The app allows you to access almost everything at the start, and then begins to restrict.
Even workouts I had previously saved now ask you to pay.
now everything I click on they want you to subscribe and that sucks
Just about everything now requires a subscription.
you can’t get anything without a subscription
since the beginning of may all of them require the subscription
couldn’t access anything without the subscription
Got this app because it was free, now you have to pay for a subscription to do the exercises.
It looks like it has amazing workout and choices for different people but you have to pay to subscribe.
Paywall to use what a disapppointment once you have reached 'free access limit'
assumptions: Users feel misled because the app initially offered free access and later restricted it.
The shift may have been communicated poorly or not at all.
This monetization strategy alienates long-time users and harms brand trust.
uncertainty_notes: A minority of users still find the subscription worth the cost, but the majority of reviews express dissatisfaction with the paywall.

title: Intrusive ads ruining user experience
description: Free-tier users are subjected to long, unskippable, or hard-to-close ads, including full-screen pop-ups that redirect to external sites (e.g., SHEIN) and interrupt workouts. Some ads have hidden close buttons, making the app nearly unusable without a subscription.
supporting_excerpts: While you’re working out, the app will close and the Shein website will open and this will happen multiple times without you prompting
This app punishes you for not paying with ads that are extremely hard to get out of
It was actually free with little to no annoying ads. I get it if they have to make you watch ads before each workout to make money, but they’re so excessively long, and hard to get out of.
How do you x out of ads? I have no idea.
They’ve hidden the X button so that when the 90s ad finishes, you can’t even get out of the ad without force closing the whole app.
every time i try to get my workout in it’s always buy premium or watch a 90 second add.
Long-time user frustrated by three-minute ads before each workout, feels it makes the app feel like a scam.
I get a pop up screen with no continuation option only advertising the paid version.
assumptions: The ad placement and behavior are intentional to push users toward premium subscription.
The Shein redirect may be an ad network issue or a malicious ad.
Hidden close buttons are likely a deliberate dark pattern.
uncertainty_notes: It is unclear if the Shein redirect is a bug or an ad implementation problem; nonetheless, it severely impacts usability.

title: Login and account access issues after payment
description: Several users report that after paying for a subscription, they are unable to log into their accounts. The app claims the account does not exist, or users are logged out and cannot regain access. This blocks use of paid features and customer support is often unhelpful.
supporting_excerpts: I paid for a year membership and I cannot log into the account because it says it does not exist.
cannot log back in to access my workouts and benefits of my paid subscription
Unable to access paid subscription
assumptions: The login bug is likely caused by an account synchronization issue after payment.
It results in complete loss of service despite payment, which is a critical failure.
Users may be abandoning the app due to this issue.
uncertainty_notes: Only three reviews explicitly describe this bug; it might be underreported or affect a small subset of users.

title: Declining content quality and repetitiveness
description: Long-time users, including paying subscribers, note a decline in workout quality. Newer workouts are reported as repetitive or AI-generated and lacking accuracy. Some users feel the app's content has become less thoughtful over time.
supporting_excerpts: most of the workouts are just AI slop now.
many of the newer daily workouts are the same things over and over
assumptions: A shift to AI-generated content may be reducing workout variety and accuracy.
This could be a cost-saving measure that alienates dedicated users.
The issue may affect only new workouts, not the entire library.
uncertainty_notes: Only two reviews explicitly mention declining quality; many other reviews still praise content variety. It is unclear how widespread the problem is.

title: Feature requests for improvements and integrations
description: Users have requested several enhancements: Apple Watch integration for calorie tracking, the ability to choose rep counting instead of a timer, more pregnancy-safe workouts, additional difficulty levels, more abdominal exercises, and subtle sound indicators. The requests indicate a desire for deeper personalization and better integration with other devices.
supporting_excerpts: I would recommend having more workout or exercise options though.
I really want my things be harder and some of the things that even if it’s up to all the way, it’s not that hard
there is a lack of pregnancy safe strength training, mobility and flexibility workouts
requests Apple Watch connectivity to track calories automatically
requests the option to switch from timer to rep counting for exercises
requests more subtle sound indicator options for use with other audio
assumptions: These features would improve user satisfaction and retention.
The user base is diverse, requiring a broad range of workout needs.
Lack of certain features may drive users to competing apps.
uncertainty_notes: These are explicit requests; actual demand may be higher than reported.

title: Positive effectiveness for weight loss and motivation
description: Many users report successful weight loss, increased motivation, and improved fitness using the app. The variety of workouts, ease of use, and motivational features are frequently praised. The app is particularly recommended for beginners and women in various life stages (e.g., postpartum, 50+).
supporting_excerpts: This app is the best. I definitely recommend to people who want to start.
I already lost 80 pounds I recommend this app a lot.
This app has everything you need to get in shape and be healthy.
Workout for Women from home and gym it’s great for beginners and it’s not killer or time consuming.
Love the easy every day consistency. The daily quotes help me with my mental health and keep me motivated.
This app customizes your workouts based off needs, age, weight, and any struggles that you face.
I started using this app when I was in the Marines and am now using it postpartum! I still love it!
Most useful app I’ve ever downloaded! Wide variety of workouts for every level.
assumptions: The app effectively meets the needs of its target audience when used consistently.
Positive outcomes drive word-of-mouth recommendations.
uncertainty_notes: Positive reviews may be from highly engaged users; dropout rates are unknown.
